segments.text: Hear ye the word which the LORD speaketh unto you, O house of Israel:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Learn not the way of the heathen, and be not dismayed at the signs of heaven; for the heathen are dismayed at them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For the customs of the people are vain:
one cutteth a tree out of the forest, the work of the hands of the workman, with the axe.

footnotes: customs…: Heb. statutes, or, ordinances are vanity

segments.text: They deck it with silver and with gold; they fasten it with nails and with hammers, that it move not.
They are upright as the palm tree, but speak not: they must needs be borne, because they cannot go.
Be not afraid of them; for they cannot do evil, neither also is it in them to do good.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Forasmuch as there is none like unto thee, O LORD; thou art great, and thy name is great in might.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Who would not fear thee, O King of nations? for to thee doth it appertain: forasmuch as among all the wise men of the nations, and in all their kingdoms, there is none like unto thee.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: But they are altogether brutish and foolish: the stock is a doctrine of vanities.

footnotes: altogether: Heb. in one, or, at once

segments.text: Silver spread into plates is brought from Tarshish, and gold from Uphaz, the work of the workman, and of the hands of the founder: blue and purple is their clothing: they are all the work of cunning men.
But the LORD is the true God, he is the living God, and an everlasting king: at his wrath the earth shall tremble, and the nations shall not be able to abide his indignation.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thus shall ye say unto them, The gods that have not made the heavens and the earth, even they shall perish from the earth, and from under these heavens.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heavens by his discretion.

When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.
Every man is brutish in his knowledge: every founder is confounded by the graven image: for his molten image is falsehood, and there is no breath in them.

footnotes: brutish in his knowledge: or, more brutish than to know

segments.text: They are vanity, and the work of errors: in the time of their visitation they shall perish.
The portion of Jacob is not like them: for he is the former of all things; and Israel is the rod of his inheritance: The LORD of hosts is his name.
Gather up thy wares out of the land, O inhabitant of the fortress.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Behold, I will sling out the inhabitants of the land at this once, and will distress them, that they may find it so.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Woe is me for my hurt! my wound is grievous: but I said, Truly this is a grief, and I must bear it.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: My tabernacle is spoiled, and all my cords are broken: my children are gone forth of me, and they are not: there is none to stretch forth my tent any more, and to set up my curtains.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: For the pastors are become brutish, and have not sought the LORD: therefore they shall not prosper, and all their flocks shall be scattered.
Behold, the noise of the bruit is come, and a great commotion out of the north country, to make the cities of Judah desolate, and a den of dragons.
O LORD, I know that the way of man is not in himself: it is not in man that walketh to direct his steps.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: O LORD, correct me, but with judgment; not in thine anger, lest thou bring me to nothing.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Pour out thy fury upon the heathen that know thee not, and upon the families that call not on thy name: for they have eaten up Jacob, and devoured him, and consumed him, and have made his habitation desolate.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah
